task_completion.description: Create an equilateral triangle with vertices at A(0,0), B(6,0), and C(3, 5.2).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a triangle in the GeoGebra graphics view with vertices positioned at the coordinates (0,0), (6,0), and approximately (3, 5.2). The Algebra view should confirm these points.

task_completion.description: Include a free, draggable point P inside the triangle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a distinct point, ideally labeled 'P', located strictly inside the boundaries of the equilateral triangle.

task_completion.description: Construct perpendicular segments from point P to all three sides of the triangle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three line segments originating from point P and intersecting the three sides of the triangle at right angles. Right-angle markers or perpendicular line definitions in the Algebra view should be visible.

task_completion.description: Measure the lengths of the three perpendicular segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for numerical values displayed next to the perpendicular segments in the graphics view, or distance/length variables listed in the Algebra view corresponding to these segments.

task_completion.description: Add a text annotation displaying the sum of these three distances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box on the GeoGebra canvas that calculates or displays the sum of the three perpendicular distances. The sum should be approximately 5.2 (or 5.196).

Look for the filename 'viviani_theorem.ggb' in the GeoGebra window title bar, or a file manager/terminal screenshot showing the file successfully saved in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoGebra graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with visible tools, algebra view, and graphics view being actively used.

integrity.description: The perpendiculars and measurements are genuine GeoGebra objects, not just drawn lines.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show dependent objects (e.g., perpendicular lines, intersect points, segments, distance calculations) rather than just independent line segments drawn to look perpendicular.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by simply writing a pre-made .ggb XML file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a history of GUI interactions, tool selections, and incremental construction steps in the screenshots, rather than a sudden appearance of the finished file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The equilateral triangle has vertices at A(0,0), B(6,0), and C(3, 5.2). The side length is exactly 6.0, and the altitude is approximately 5.196. According to Viviani's Theorem, the sum of the perpendicular distances from any interior point P to the three sides must equal the triangle's altitude (approx 5.196 or 5.2). The final file must be named 'viviani_theorem.ggb'.
